data.title: Hofseth Biocare Mulls CHF8 Million Bond Issuance
data.description:
Hofseth Biocare is considering the issuance of up to 8 million francs of unsecured bonds with a three-year tenor.

The Norwegian consumer and pet health company mandated Novum Asset Management for the potential issue targeting investors in Switzerland and Liechtenstein, according to a Monday release.

data.title: Mineralys surges as blood pressure drug meets trial goals
data.description:
** Drug developer Mineralys Therapeutics' shares surge 50.42% to $15.82 premarket

** MLYS says its experimental drug, lorundrostat, met the main goals of mid-and late-stage trials

** In the late-stage trial, co's drug reduced blood pressure by 9.1 mmHg (millimeters of mercury) at 6 weeks, compared with placebo

** Lorundrostat inhibits production of aldosterone, a hormone that can contribute to high blood pressure

** In the mid-stage trial, the drug reduced blood pressure by placebo-adjusted 7.9 mmHg

** Stock has fallen 24.7% in the last 12 months

data.title: Brent Crude Extends Uptick
data.description:
Brent crude oil futures rose past the $70.7 mark on Monday, inching higher since touching the six-month low of $69.3 touched on March 5th amid a weaker dollar and bargain hunting by energy traders following a batch of bearish factors for energy demand.

The outlook for fuel consumption continued to be hampered by escalating trade wars between major oil consumers and producers, with the US, China, and Canada exchanging tariffs and retaliatory levies between each other.

Aggressive measures by the US drove President Trump and Treasury Secretary Bessent to both signal the possibility of economic hardship.

This magnified the impact of the agreement by OPEC+ to proceed with oil output hikes as early as April following members' difficulty in meeting their output cut pledges.

Additionally, fresh data from top fuel importer China showed that consumer and producer prices fell more than expected in February, raising concerns of low consumption despite higher stimulus and credit growth.

data.title: Wolfe Research Adjusts Domino's Pizza Price Target to $525 From $508, Maintains Outperform Rating
data.description: Domino's Pizza has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $493.90, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Evolent Health Insider Bought Shares Worth $273,750, According to a Recent SEC Filing
data.description:
Richard M Jelinek, Director, on March 07, 2025, executed a purchase for 30,000 shares in Evolent Health for $273,750. Following the Form 4 filing with the SEC, Jelinek has control over a total of 44,458 Class A common shares of the company, with 14,458 shares held directly and 30,000 controlled indirectly.

SEC Filing:

www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1628908/000162890825000048/xslF345X05/wk-form4_1741608974.xml

data.title: Neo Energy Metals Unit Signs Ancillary Acquisition Deal for South African Uranium Project
data.description:
Neo Energy Metals' 70%-owned Neo Uranium Resources Beisa Mine entered a sales deal with Sibanye Stillwater (SSW.JO) unit Sibanye Gold Proprietary for 6,244 hectares of land in the Free State province of South Africa.

The ancillary acquisition deal covers the Beisa uranium project's mining right area as well as associated assets where the mining right extends over, according to a Monday filing.

The uranium mining company noted the transaction's consideration is already included in Neo Uranium's acquisition of the Beisa project and that no additional payments are needed.

data.title: Tariff and growth worries boost safe-haven yen, Swiss franc
data.description:
Key points:
  • Investors buy safe-haven yen and Swiss franc
  • Yen hovers at five-month high
  • Euro firm after best week in 16 years

The Japanese yen and Swiss franc strengthened against the dollar on Monday, as traders flocked to safe-haven currencies due to lingering worries over tariffs and a U.S. economic slowdown, while the euro added to gains after a strong run last week.

The yen on Monday was 0.7% firmer at 147.035 per dollar, having reached 146.94 on Friday, its strongest in five months.

The Swiss franc reached 0.87635 per dollar, its highest in three months.

Markets have been fixated on trade tensions after U.S. President Donald Trump slapped tariffs on top trading partners only to delay some of them for a month amid fears of a U.S. economic slowdown.

That has led some investors to lose faith in the U.S. economy which has hitherto been outperforming its peers.

On currency futures markets, investors have slashed net long dollar positions to $15.3 billion from a nine-year high of $35.2 billion in January.

There are also domestic factors at play in Japan.

"We still favour the JPY to outperform as we think wage discussions ... should solidify the wage-inflation cycle that will keep the BOJ on a hawkish path ahead," said Dominic Bunning, global forex strategist at Nomura.

Data on Monday showed regular pay in Japan rose 3.1% in January following December's revised 2.6% increase and marking the biggest jump since 1992, though inflation at a two-year high meant real wages fell.

The Bank of Japan is widely expected to keep interest rates unchanged at its policy review on March 18-19, though officials have repeatedly cited the need to gauge the sustainability of wage growth after the central bank's January rate hike.

FOLLOW THROUGH

Following a volatile week that saw the euro's biggest weekly gain since 2009, the common currency edged 0.18% higher against the dollar and hovered near its four-month high as the markets awaited details on the likely boost to European spending.

"We've seen a lot of news from Germany on defence and infrastructure, but the feeling is that there's going to be some follow through," said Samy Chaar, chief economist at Lombard Odier.

European Union finance ministers will meet on Monday to explore funding options for defence. European countries have rushed to boost spending and maintain support for Ukraine after Trump froze U.S. military aid to Kyiv and raised doubts about Washington's commitment to European allies.

Meanwhile, a survey showed on Monday that investor morale in the euro zone brightened substantially in March, with economic expectations hitting their highest reading since July 2021, boosted by Germany's fiscal reforms.

EYES ON INFLATION

Investors were also digesting data from Friday that showed U.S. job growth picked up in February, but cracks are emerging in the once-resilient labour market amid a chaotic trade policy.

Eyes will now be on U.S. inflation data due on Wednesday.

Traders are pricing in 75 basis points of cuts from the Fed this year, LSEG data showed, with a rate cut fully priced in for June.

In other currencies, the Norwegian crown gained against the dollar and the euro. It was at its strongest against the dollar since October at 10.7144 crowns to the U.S. currency after surging inflation sowed doubts about the central bank's plans to start cutting borrowing costs in March. ,

China's yuan slipped on Monday after data over the weekend showed the consumer price index in February fell at the sharpest pace in 13 months.

The British pound steadied at $1.2923 ahead of monthly GDP data later this week.

data.title: Trade Jitters Hit Investor Sentiment, Driving Premarket Losses for US Equity Futures
data.description:
US equity futures were lower pre-bell Monday as traders monitored developments surrounding US President Donald Trump's evolving trade policy amid reports of a possible slowing of the US economy.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average futures fell 1%, S&P 500 futures decreased 1.2%, and Nasdaq futures were down 1.4%.

On Friday, Trump issued a fresh threat to impose tariffs on Canadian dairy and lumber products—the latest in a roller-coaster week of trade policy announcements.

Meanwhile, concerns that tariff policies could hit the country's economic activity increased after Trump declined to rule out a possible recession, according to media reports.

Oil prices were higher, with front-month global benchmark North Sea Brent crude up 0.5% at $70.74 per barrel and US West Texas Intermediate crude 0.6% higher at $67.43 per barrel.

There are no major economic data due for release.

data.title: Aligos Therapeutics, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Aligos Therapeutics, Inc., a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing novel therapeutics for liver diseases and viral infections, has released its annual Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the highly competitive biopharmaceutical industry.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue from collaborations: $0.3 million, a significant decrease from the previous year due to the completion of the Original Agreement with Merck.
  • Revenue from customers: $3.6 million, a decrease primarily due to the agreement with Amoytop signed in 2023.
  • Loss from operations: $(89.2) million, reflecting a slight increase in loss compared to the previous year.
  • Net loss: $(131.2) million, a substantial increase from the previous year, primarily due to a significant change in the fair value of common warrants.
  • Net loss per share, basic and diluted: $(20.94), reflecting the increased net loss and changes in the number of shares outstanding.

Business Highlights

  • Pipeline Overview: The company is focused on developing novel therapeutics for liver diseases and viral infections, with a pipeline that includes ALG-000184 for chronic HBV infection, ALG-055009 for MASH, and ALG-097558 for coronavirus infections.
  • Chronic HBV Infection: ALG-000184, a Capsid Assembly Modulator, has shown superior viral suppression and potential to replace standard NA treatments. It is in a multi-part Phase 1 clinical trial with promising results in viral DNA and antigen reduction.
  • MASH Treatment: ALG-055009, a THR-β agonist, demonstrated significant liver fat reduction in a Phase 2a study, with potential cardiovascular benefits. It is being prepared for Phase 2b studies.
  • Coronavirus Treatment: ALG-097558, a small molecule protease inhibitor, is 3-fold more potent than existing treatments in cell-based assays and does not require ritonavir boosting. It is in clinical trials for high-risk COVID-19 patients.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue developing its drug candidates, with ongoing and future clinical trials for ALG-000184, ALG-055009, and ALG-097558. They are also exploring potential out-licensing and external funding opportunities.
  • Research and Development Strategy: The company leverages its expertise in small molecule and oligonucleotide platforms to discover and develop potentially best-in-class regimens for liver and viral diseases.
  • Management and Expertise: The management team has extensive experience in drug discovery and development, with a track record of success in commercializing drugs for liver and viral diseases.
  • Intellectual Property: The company holds a robust patent portfolio, with patents expected to expire between 2040 and 2044, providing protection for its drug candidates and technologies.
  • Collaborations and Licensing: The company has entered into various licensing agreements with institutions like Emory University and Luxna Biotech to enhance its drug discovery and development capabilities.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Focus: Aligos Therapeutics is focused on advancing its clinical-stage drug candidates, including ALG-000184 for chronic HBV, ALG-055009 for MASH, and ALG-097558 for coronavirus infections. The company is actively pursuing collaborations and strategic alliances to support its research and development efforts, as evidenced by its partnerships with institutions like the Rega Institute and the National Institutes of Health.
  • Capital Management: The company has engaged in several capital management activities, including a PIPE offering in October 2023, which raised $86.2 million in net proceeds, and a subsequent private placement in February 2025, generating approximately $105.0 million. These funds are intended to support ongoing research and development activities. Aligos has also managed its cash resources by investing in short-term investments and maintaining a focus on reducing operating expenses, as seen in the decrease in general and administrative costs. The company has not declared any dividends and has not engaged in equity repurchases.
  • Future Outlook: Aligos plans to continue its strategic focus on developing its pipeline of drug candidates, with ongoing clinical trials and research activities expected to require substantial funding. The company anticipates further capital raising activities, including potential public or private equity offerings, to support its long-term objectives. Aligos is also seeking additional external funding, such as governmental grants, to advance its coronavirus program and other initiatives. The company aims to maintain its liquidity position to fund operations for at least the next 12 months, while exploring opportunities for strategic partnerships and collaborations to enhance its research capabilities.

Challenges and Risks

  • Limited Operating History: The company faces significant risks due to its limited operating history as a clinical-stage biotechnology company with no products approved for commercial sale. This lack of revenue generation from product sales and the expectation of continued losses pose a challenge to achieving profitability.
  • Dependence on Drug Development: The company relies heavily on the successful development and commercialization of its drug candidates, which are still in early stages of clinical trials. The need for substantial additional financing to support ongoing research and development activities is critical, and there is no guarantee that such funding will be available on acceptable terms.
  • Industry Risks: The biopharmaceutical industry is highly speculative and involves substantial risks, including the potential for significant delays or failures in clinical trials, regulatory approvals, and commercialization efforts. The company has not yet demonstrated the ability to complete clinical trials, obtain marketing approvals, or manufacture and commercialize products at scale.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including competition from other companies with more resources and established products.
  • Operational Risks: Operational risks include the potential impact of health pandemics or epidemics, such as COVID-19, which could disrupt clinical trials, supply chains, and overall business operations.
  • Regulatory Risks: The company faces regulatory risks, as the approval processes of the FDA and other authorities are lengthy, complex, and inherently unpredictable. Changes in regulatory requirements or policies could further delay or prevent the approval of drug candidates.
  • International Risks: The company is also subject to risks related to its international operations, including different regulatory requirements, economic instability, and potential disruptions from geopolitical events.
  • Third-Party Risks: The reliance on third-party manufacturers and suppliers adds another layer of risk, as any disruptions in these relationships could impact the company's ability to conduct clinical trials and commercialize products.
  • Financial Position: Management acknowledges the challenges posed by the company's financial position and the need for additional capital to achieve its goals. The company plans to finance its operations through a combination of public or private equity offerings, debt financings, and strategic collaborations.
  • Market Risk Exposure: The company is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates, which could adversely affect its financial results. Additionally, the company faces risks related to changes in interest rates and the availability of capital, which could impact its ability to finance ongoing operations and strategic initiatives.

SEC Filing: Aligos Therapeutics, Inc. [ ALGS ] - 10-K - Mar. 10, 2025

data.title: Perion Network Increases Buyback Program to $125 Million; Shares Rise Pre-Bell
data.description:
Perion Network said Monday that its board has authorized a $75 million increase to the company's $50 million buyback program to raise the total to $125 million.

The tech company said that as of the end of 2024, it has repurchased 5.2 million shares worth $46.8 million.

Shares of Perion Network were up 3.9% in recent premarket activity.

data.title: Travis Perkins's CEO Pete Redfern to Step Down Due to Ill Health
data.description:
(12:10 GMT) *Travis Perkins: Redfern Will Step Down With Immediate Effect
(12:11 GMT) *Travis Perkins: Nominations Committee Will Immediately Search for New CEO
(12:19 GMT) Travis Perkins's CEO Pete Redfern to Step Down Due to Ill Health

By Cristina Gallardo

Travis Perkins said its Chief Executive Officer Pete Redfern will step down due to ill health with immediate effect.

The London-listed company said Monday that its board had accepted Redfern's resignation, after just half a year leading the builders' merchant and home improvement retailer group.

The company's chairman Geoff Drabble will work with the management team to ensure the stability of the business, while the nominations committee focuses on the search of a successor, Travis Perkins said.

Write to Cristina Gallardo at cristina.gallardo@wsj.com

data.title: There's a Fed put and a Trump put - but don't forget the C-suite put as well
data.description:
By Jamie Chisholm

Buybacks may hit $1 trillion this year as companies switch from capex

Stock market optimists often proclaim that equities are protected by various puts. In option parlance, buying a put protects you from a fall in an asset price.

The "Fed put" means that if a diving market threatens to damage the economy via the wealth effect then the U.S. central bank will cut borrowing costs. The "Trump put" hoped that the new administration would dial back its destabilizing policies if the market feared a sharp economic downturn.

Can we now add the "C-suite put?" That's when economic uncertainty discourages corporate capital expenditure and as equities fall encourages instead more share buybacks.

That's what analysts at Citi led by Scott Chronert think may happen.

In a note published Monday, the Citi team said: "[A]n analysis of S&P 500 capex and buyback trends supports our ongoing constructive structural view on the index. Cash flow deployment could incrementally shift from capex to buybacks should further equity price weakness unfold."

At the moment strategists are positive on corporate investment. S&P 500 SPX capex is forecast to increase by 7% this year, having jumped 13% in 2024, said Citi. Much of the spending is led by big tech, with the Magnificent 7 stocks expected to contribute nearly 32% of projected 2025 capex.

Corporate optimism saw executives reallocate some cash flow use from buybacks to capex in 2024, according to Citi. This can be seen in the table below, for example, where growth companies trimmed buybacks by 3.6% and increased capex by 3.5%.

The Citi team say Trump administration policy risks, particularly related to tariffs, are not properly reflected in 2025 consensus company earnings expectations.

"Simply, ongoing uncertainty as to magnitude and duration of tariffs makes it difficult for analysts to project impacts with much precision for single stocks let alone at the index level," said Citi.

"Ultimately, the Trump policy path toward reshoring and related infrastructure...should, in theory, lead to higher capex spend. But, at current, it is hard to see that forest through the trees," they add.

This mean that there is a risk capex, in the short to medium-term, will be less than forecasts. And if that happens, managers will be reluctant to invest for growth and may instead look to take advantage of falling share prices.

Aggregate buybacks for the S&P 500 were nearly $900 billion in 2024. Citi think that level is achievable this year, and it could rise to $1 trillion if equity markets continue to be under pressure.

"Essentially, lower stock prices should, at the company specific level, cause C-suites to reconsider cash deployment. This is predicated, intuitively, on lower stock prices reflecting, at least to some degree, economic/macro uncertainty," said Citi.

This would help stock markets find a floor, Citi said. "A -10% retrenchment from the February 19 highs would take the S&P 500 toward the 5500 level. Based on our current fundamental expectations this would provide an attractive risk/reward setup relative to our current 6500 base case," the bank said.

Related: Here's the level that could be an attractive entry point for stocks, says Morgan Stanley's Mike Wilson

-Jamie Chisholm

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Evolent Health Insider Bought Shares Worth $497,577, According to a Recent SEC Filing
data.description:
Seth Blackley, Director, Chief Executive Officer, on March 06, 2025, executed a purchase for 55,225 shares in Evolent Health for $497,577. Following the Form 4 filing with the SEC, Blackley has control over a total of 762,217 Class A common shares of the company, with 762,217 shares held directly.

data.title: Tak Lee Machinery Anticipates Up to 70% Higher Profit for Six Months to January
data.description:
Tak Lee Machinery Holdings expects to log 60% to 70% higher net profit for the six months ended Jan. 31, compared with the same period in 2024, a Monday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The earthmoving equipment and spare parts company attributed the expected increase to a rise in sales and leasing of heavy equipment during the reporting period resulting from increased demand.

Shares of the company closed over 4% higher on Monday.

data.title: Wayfair Unit to Offer $700 Million in Senior Notes
data.description:
Wayfair said Monday that its unit, Wayfair LLC, plans to offer $700 million in senior secured notes due 2030 in a private offering.

The company said the net proceeds will be used to purchase a portion of its outstanding 0.625% convertible senior notes due 2025 and 1% convertible senior notes due 2026, as well as for general corporate purposes, which may include the repayment of its debt.

Wayfair said it also plans to enter into an amended and restated credit agreement to establish a new credit facility, which it expects will extend the credit facilities' maturity to 2030 and allow for commitments of up to $500 million.

data.title: WTI Inches Higher for 2nd Session
data.description:
WTI crude oil futures rose past the $67 mark on Monday, inching higher since touching the near-two-year low of $66.4 touched on March 6th amid a weaker dollar and bargain hunting by energy traders following a batch of bearish factors for energy demand.

The outlook for fuel consumption continued to be hampered by escalating trade wars between major oil consumers and producers, with the US, China, and Canada exchanging tariffs and retaliatory levies between each other.

Aggressive measures by the US drove President Trump and Treasury Secretary Bessent to both signal the possibility of economic hardship.

This magnified the impact of the agreement by OPEC+ to proceed with oil output hikes as early as April following members' difficulty in meeting their output cut pledges.

Additionally, fresh data from top fuel importer China showed that consumer and producer prices fell more than expected in February, raising concerns of low consumption despite higher stimulus and credit growth.

data.title: Citigroup Initiates Karman Holdings at Buy With $42 Price Target
Baird Initiates Karman Holdings at Outperform With $40 Price Target
UK's Travis Perkins CEO to step down due to ill health
data.description: British buildings materials supplier Travis Perkins said on Monday its CEO Pete Redfern was stepping down due to ill health.

data.title: Mineralys Therapeutics Says Lorundrostat Met Trial Endpoints for Treating Hypertension; Shares Up Pre-Bell
data.description:
Mineralys Therapeutics shares were up premarket Monday after the company said lorundrostat met primary endpoints in both the phase 3 Launch-HTN and phase 2 Advance-HTN trials in patients with uncontrolled hypertension or resistant hypertension.

The company said the phase 3 study showed a statistically significant drop in systolic blood pressure compared with placebo by week six until week 12.

In the phase 2 trial, lorundrostat also led to a statistically significant placebo-adjusted drop of 7.9 mmHg in systolic pressure at week 12, Mineralys said.

Mineralys said the treatment was generally well tolerated in both studies, with low rates of serious side effects and elevated potassium levels. The company said it plans to present more data at a medical conference later this month and continue collecting longer-term results in an extension study.

data.title: Kilroy Realty Cut to Equal-Weight From Overweight by Wells Fargo
data.description: (12:13 GMT) Kilroy Realty Price Target Cut to $35.00/Share From $43.00 by Wells Fargo

data.title: Beam Therapeutics Prices $500 Million Share, Warrant Offering
data.description:
Beam Therapeutics said Monday it has priced an underwritten offering of 16.2 million shares at $28.48 per share, and pre-funded warrants to purchase 1.4 million shares at $28.47 per pre-funded warrant.

Gross proceeds from the offering, expected to close Tuesday, are anticipated to be about $500.0 million, the company said.

Beam said intends to use net proceeds from the offering for technology advancement, research and development, and other general corporate purposes.
